title: Elaphoglossum calophyllum
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum calophyllum is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and the order Polypodiales. This species is further categorized under the family Polypodiaceae and the genus Elaphoglossum. Elaphoglossum calophyllum is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its environment.
uses: Elaphoglossum calophyllum is a species of fern that has been found to have various medicinal properties. It is traditionally used in some cultures to treat ailments such as fever and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems are often used in teas and infusions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential as a medicinal resource. However, its traditional uses demonstrate its potential value in folk medicine.

The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections.
